analysis.context: A scene from a war drama where a newly arrived, inexperienced officer volunteers for a patrol but is denied by his superiors.

0-5.transcript: -Who are you? -Lt. Jones, sir. Right, our West Pointer.
0-5.technical_description: Medium shot of two soldiers talking, camera is slightly shaky.

5-9.transcript: -Yes, sir. -When did you graduate? June 6, sir.
5-9.technical_description: Close-up on the younger soldier, then cuts to the older one. Clean shot.

9-14.transcript: June 6? Of last year? D-Day, yes, sir.
9-14.technical_description: Medium shot of the older soldier reacting, then close-up of the younger one. Clean shot.

14-18.technical_description: Medium shot of the older soldier laughing and walking away. Clean shot.

18-23.transcript: Sir, I'd like to volunteer for the patrol.
18-23.technical_description: Close-up of the younger soldier speaking. Clean shot.

23-27.transcript: -Speirs, talk to you in an hour. -Yeah. Lt. Jones.
23-27.technical_description: Medium shot of soldiers exchanging brief words. Clean shot.

27-32.transcript: We're short on officers. You think a non-com could lead this?
27-32.technical_description: Close-up of the younger soldier listening to the conversation off-screen. Clean shot.

32-36.transcript: I can think of a few possibilities.
32-36.technical_description: Medium shot of two soldiers sitting and discussing. Clean shot.

36-41.transcript: Martin? Malarkey? Grant? Honestly, sir, most of the NCOs could use a rest.
36-41.technical_description: Close-up of a soldier explaining the situation. Clean shot.

41-46.transcript: Captain. Request permission to go on the patrol. There's your answer.
41-46.technical_description: Close-up of the younger soldier making a request, then a quick cut to another soldier. Clean shot.

46-49.transcript: No. You don't have any experience.
46-49.technical_description: Close-up of the commanding officer denying the request. Clean shot.

49-53.technical_description: Close-up of the younger soldier accepting orders. Clean shot.
